<insights>

- Isolated files — no import edges between 4 files, cohesion is unmeasurable (reported as 0)
- These files are generated and mutated at runtime — treat them as a data layer, not source code, when reasoning about architecture.
- The execution-log.jsonl and workflow.md files provide audit trail and operator visibility into PRD state changes.
- Config, PRD tree, and log are co-located here, which makes backup and version-control snapshots of the full project state straightforward.
- Zone contains only data files (JSON, JSONL, Markdown) with no source imports or entry points — cohesion/coupling metrics are not meaningful here and should not trigger restructuring.
- prd.json and config.json serve distinct roles (domain state vs. project settings) and are already separated, which prevents config drift from corrupting PRD data.
- Absence from all 15 cross-zone import edges confirms this zone is a true data-only layer — it is never imported by source code, only written and read by the process at runtime.
- rex-runtime-state does not appear on either side of any cross-zone import edge, confirming it is a pure data layer with no source coupling. This is the correct relationship for runtime-generated files and should be preserved.
